area_description: RAG refers to providing grounded information by retrieving from external sources, including web sources, knowledge graphs, and documents. The retrieved information is then used to answer questions or generate responses that are relevant to the user's query.

categories.name: Modularized RAG Pipeline
categories.description: A modular RAG pipeline containing distinct stages: RAG triggering, query rewriting, retrieval, post-processing, and answer generation, with a special case of embedding concatenation.

sub_topics.name: RAG Triggering
sub_topics.description: Deciding whether to trigger the RAG pipeline, including trigger-for-all or trigger-for-real-time-questions approaches.

sub_topics.name: Query Rewriting
sub_topics.description: Generating the web search query, or query for particular data sources like knowledge graphs and documents.

sub_topics.name: Retrieval
sub_topics.description: Retrieving from potentially multiple types of sources, including retrieval and ranking.

sub_topics.name: Post-processing
sub_topics.description: Chunking retrieval results, re-ranking and filtering on the chunks.

sub_topics.name: Answer Generation
sub_topics.description: Generating answers that are resilient to retrieval noises.

sub_topics.name: Embedding Concatenation
sub_topics.description: Special case where embeddings are concatenated instead of texts for retrieval augmentation.

categories.name: Graph-based RAG Pipeline
categories.description: Constructing a knowledge graph from the corpus and leveraging the graph to enable reasoning for retrieval-augmented generation.

categories.name: Agentic RAG Pipeline
categories.description: Iteratively deciding whether to retrieve and how to augment retrieval results during decoding, with retrieval interleaved at the decoding phase.

theme.name: Complex Question
theme.description: Answering complex questions that require aggregating multiple pieces of information and potentially issuing multiple searches.

theme.name: Analysis
theme.description: Papers where experiments are conducted to evaluate the performance of baseline solutions, showing gaps and future directions.

theme.name: Benchmark
theme.description: Papers that introduce new benchmark datasets and evaluation metrics.

theme.name: Application
theme.description: Papers that show how to apply the techniques to a specific domain or task, highlighting its strengths and gaps.

theme.name: Survey
theme.description: Papers that provide a comprehensive overview of the current state of the art, including its taxonomy, advantages and limitations.
